2026-03-10.title: West Bengal SIR test: reading the Supreme Court’s order
2026-03-10.title_hi: पश्चिम बंगाल में चुनावी मतदाता सूची विवाद: सुप्रीम कोर्ट के आदेश की व्याख्या
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Polity: Election Commission and Electoral Process
2026-03-10.context: The Supreme Court's order comes amidst 60 lakh pending cases in West Bengal, which has sparked a discussion on the Election Commission's need for permanent staff to handle electoral roll disputes. The publication of final rolls is also a crucial aspect of this issue. The Supreme Court's decision aims to address the backlog of cases and ensure the integrity of the electoral process. The Election Commission's current staffing structure has been questioned, with some arguing that it needs permanent staff to effectively manage electoral disputes. The Supreme Court's order is seen as a step towards strengthening the electoral process in West Bengal.
key_points: The Supreme Court — Electoral Roll Disputes: Ordered deployment of judicial officers to decide cases.
Election Commission — Staffing: Equipped with permanent staff to handle electoral roll disputes.
West Bengal — Pending Cases: 60 lakh cases pending adjudication.
The Supreme Court — Publication of Final Rolls: Crucial aspect of electoral process.
Election Commission — Electoral Process: Aims to ensure integrity and transparency.

2026-03-10.title: Prime Minister to launch projects worth ₹10,800 crore at Kochi event
2026-03-10.title_hi: प्रधान मंत्री ₹10,800 करोड़ की परियोजनाओं का कोच्चि में शुभारंभ करेंगे
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Governance: Government Schemes and Initiatives
2026-03-10.context: The Prime Minister will lay the foundation stone for a polypropylene unit at Kochi Refinery, a project worth ₹10,800 crore. This initiative is expected to boost the region's infrastructure and economy. The event will also see the inauguration of 23 rural roads constructed under the Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana in the State. The Kochi Refinery is a significant contributor to the region's industrial growth. The Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana aims to improve rural connectivity across the country.
key_points: The Prime Minister — Polypropylene Unit: Laying foundation stone at Kochi Refinery (₹10,800 crore).
The Prime Minister — Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana: Inaugurating 23 rural roads in the State.
The Government — Kochi Refinery: Boosting regional infrastructure and economy through the polypropylene unit.
The Government — Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana: Improving rural connectivity across the country.
The Prime Minister — Kochi Event: Launching projects worth ₹10,800 crore.

2026-03-10.title: Supreme Court approves Centre's proposal extending term of tribunal members
2026-03-10.title_hi: सुप्रीम कोर्ट ने केंद्र के प्रस्ताव को मंजूरी दी, ट्रिब्यूनल सदस्यों की अवधि बढ़ाई
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Polity: Judiciary and Tribunal Reforms
2026-03-10.context: The Supreme Court has given its nod to the Centre's proposal to extend the term of tribunal members. This move will affect those appointed under the Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021. According to Attorney General R Venkataramani, a new law is likely to be enacted by next September, either in the upcoming Budget session or the Monsoon session. Around 21 members are due to retire before the new law is enacted. The extension is intended to ensure continuity until the new legislation is in place.
key_points: Supreme Court — Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021: Approved extension of term for tribunal members.
Centre — Proposal: Extended term for tribunal members until new law is enacted.
Attorney General R Venkataramani — Statement: New law likely to be enacted by next September.
Tribunal Members — Retirement: Around 21 members due to retire before new law is enacted.
Centre — Legislation: Aims to ensure continuity and address issues arising from Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021.

2026-03-10.title: Israel-Iran war: Trump says war will end 'very soon'; U.S. saving some ‘most important’ targets for later
2026-03-10.title_hi: इज़राइल-इरान संघर्ष: ट्रंप ने कहा जल्द ही युद्ध समाप्त हो जाएगा
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — International Relations: Middle East Conflict and US Foreign Policy
2026-03-10.context: The US President played down the scale of the conflict, which has not been approved by the US Congress, repeatedly calling it an 'excursion' rather than a war. The conflict between Israel and Iran has been escalating, with the US involved in the situation. Trump's statement comes amid rising tensions in the region. The US Congress has not approved the conflict, raising questions about the legitimacy of US involvement. The situation is being closely watched by the international community.
key_points: US President — Statement: Said the war will end 'very soon'.
US Congress — Approval: Has not approved the conflict.
US President — Description: Repeatedly called the conflict an 'excursion' rather than a war.
International Community — Response: Is closely watching the situation.
US — Involvement: Has been involved in the conflict, with its actions being questioned by the US Congress.

2026-03-10.title: UP govt signs pact with Hinduja Group firm to expand broadband connectivity
2026-03-10.title_hi: उत्तर प्रदेश सरकार ने हिंदुजा ग्रुप की कंपनी के साथ ब्रॉडबैंड कनेक्टिविटी विस्तार के लिए समझौता किया
2026-03-10.context: The Uttar Pradesh government has signed a pact with a Hinduja Group firm to expand broadband connectivity in the state. The pact aims to provide high-speed internet services to around 3.5 lakh villages and 20,000 gram panchayats. The project is expected to be completed within the next 2 years at a cost of ₹2,500 crore. The Hinduja Group firm will be responsible for laying optical fibre cables across the state. The project is expected to benefit around 16 crore people in the state.
key_points: Uttar Pradesh government — Broadband Expansion Project: Signed pact with Hinduja Group firm to expand broadband connectivity.
Hinduja Group firm — Broadband Expansion Project: Will lay optical fibre cables across the state at a cost of ₹2,500 crore.
Uttar Pradesh government — Broadband Expansion Project: Aims to provide high-speed internet services to around 3.5 lakh villages and 20,000 gram panchayats.
Uttar Pradesh government — Broadband Expansion Project: Expected to benefit around 16 crore people in the state.
Uttar Pradesh government — Broadband Expansion Project: Expected to be completed within the next 2 years.

2026-03-11.title: Union Cabinet approves international status for Madurai airport
2026-03-11.title_hi: केंद्रीय मंत्रिमंडल ने मदुरै हवाई अड्डे को अंतरराष्ट्रीय दर्जा दिया
2026-03-11.context: The Union Cabinet has approved the international status for Madurai airport, which is expected to boost the airport's connectivity with foreign countries. This move is likely to increase the number of regular international routes and make it easier for foreign airlines to operate from the airport. The airport currently handles domestic flights and some international charter flights. The new status is expected to increase the airport's revenue and create more job opportunities. The approval is also expected to promote tourism and trade in the region.
key_points: Union Cabinet — International Status: Approved for Madurai airport.
Airports Authority of India (AAI) — Infrastructure Upgrade: Constructing a new terminal building and expanding the runway.
Madurai Airport — International Routes: Expected to increase regular international routes.
Foreign Airlines — Approval: Easier approvals for foreign airlines to operate from the airport.
Airport Revenue — Increase: Expected to increase the airport's revenue and create more job opportunities.

2026-03-11.title: Kerala Cabinet approves new anti-narcotics bureau
2026-03-11.title_hi: केरल कैबिनेट ने नई एन्टी नार्कोटिक्स ब्यूरो को मंजूरी दी
2026-03-11.gs_paper: GS2 — Governance: Government Schemes and Initiatives
2026-03-11.context: The Kerala Cabinet has given its nod to set up a new anti-narcotics bureau, which will be headed by an ADGP-rank officer. The bureau will have 3 units, including a narcotics intelligence unit, a narcotics investigation unit, and a narcotics prosecution unit. The state government has allocated ₹10 crore for the initial setup of the bureau. The move is aimed at curbing the increasing drug-related crimes in Kerala, which has seen a 25% rise in such cases over the past year.
key_points: Kerala Cabinet — Anti-Narcotics Bureau: Approved formation of a new bureau to tackle drug menace.
Kerala Government — Anti-Narcotics Bureau: Allocated ₹10 crore for initial setup.
Kerala Police — Anti-Narcotics Bureau: Will have 3 units, including narcotics intelligence, investigation, and prosecution units.
Kerala Government — Anti-Narcotics Bureau: Move aimed at curbing 25% rise in drug-related crimes.
Kerala Cabinet — Anti-Narcotics Bureau: Bureau to be headed by an ADGP-rank officer.

2026-03-11.title: Chhattisgarh Cabinet approves new anti-conversion law draft
2026-03-11.title_hi: छत्तीसगढ़ कैबिनेट ने नए धर्म परिवर्तन विरोधी कानून के मसौदे को मंजूरी दी
2026-03-11.context: The Chhattisgarh Cabinet has approved a draft of a new anti-conversion law, which will now be introduced in the State Assembly. The Bill aims to regulate religious conversions in the state. The approval comes during the ongoing session of the State Assembly. The law is expected to have significant implications for the state's population. The Chhattisgarh government has been working on the draft for several months.
key_points: The Chhattisgarh Cabinet — Anti-Conversion Law: Approved draft for introduction in State Assembly.
The Chhattisgarh government — Legislative Process: Introduced during ongoing State Assembly session.
The State Assembly — Legislative Agenda: Expected to discuss and vote on the Bill.
The Chhattisgarh government — Law and Order: Aims to regulate religious conversions in the state.
The State Government — Policy Initiative: Expected to have significant implications for the state's population.

2026-03-11.title: Cabinet approves two rail projects
2026-03-11.title_hi: मंत्रिमंडल ने दो रेल परियोजनाओं को मंजूरी दी
2026-03-11.gs_paper: GS2 — Governance: Government Schemes and Infrastructure Development
2026-03-11.context: The Cabinet has approved two new rail projects, which are expected to improve rail connectivity in the region. The first project involves the construction of a fourth rail line between Santragachi and Kharagpur in West Bengal, while the second project connects Sainthia in West Bengal and Pakur in Jharkhand. These projects will help in reducing congestion and increasing the speed of trains. The total cost of the projects is not specified in the summary.
key_points: The Cabinet — Rail Project: Approved a fourth rail line between Santragachi and Kharagpur in West Bengal.
The Cabinet — Rail Project: Approved a new rail line connecting Sainthia in West Bengal and Pakur in Jharkhand.
The Indian Railways — Infrastructure Development: Aims to improve rail connectivity and reduce congestion in the region.
The Indian Railways — Network Expansion: Has been focusing on increasing the speed and efficiency of trains.
The Ministry of Railways — Project Implementation: Will be responsible for the implementation and monitoring of the projects.

2026-03-11.title: Now, Rajasthan Assembly passes Bill to repeal two-child norm for ULB elections
2026-03-11.title_hi: राजस्थान विधानसभा ने दो बच्चों की नियम को निरस्त करने वाला विधेयक पारित किया
2026-03-11.context: The Rajasthan Assembly has passed a Bill to repeal the two-child norm for Urban Local Body (ULB) elections. The two-child norm was introduced in 2019, which barred individuals with more than two children from contesting ULB elections. The Bill aims to remove this restriction, allowing individuals with more than two children to participate in ULB elections. The move is expected to impact approximately 10% of the total ULB seats in the state. The Rajasthan government has stated that the decision was made to promote inclusivity and increase participation in local governance.
key_points: Rajasthan Assembly — Repeal Bill: Passed to remove two-child norm for ULB elections.
Rajasthan Government — ULB Elections: Approximately 10% of total seats expected to be impacted.
Rajasthan Panchayati Raj (Amendment) Act, 2019 — Two-Child Norm: Introduced in 2019 to promote family planning and population control.
Rajasthan Assembly — Repeal Bill: Aims to promote inclusivity and increase participation in local governance.
Rajasthan Government — ULB Elections: Decision made to remove restriction on individuals with more than two children.

2026-03-12.title: Supreme Court judge distinguishes between active and passive euthanasia
2026-03-12.title_hi: सुप्रीम कोर्ट ने एक्टिव और पैसिव यूथेनेशिया में अंतर स्पष्ट किया
2026-03-12.gs_paper: GS2 — Social Justice: Right to Life and Personal Liberty
2026-03-12.context: The Supreme Court judge explained the difference between active and passive euthanasia, stating that active euthanasia involves causing death by introducing a new, external agency of harm, such as a lethal injection. On the other hand, passive euthanasia is understood as allowing death to occur. This distinction is crucial in understanding the nuances of euthanasia. The judge's clarification comes amidst ongoing debates about the right to die with dignity. The Supreme Court has been examining the issue of euthanasia and its implications on individual rights and medical ethics.
key_points: Supreme Court — Euthanasia: Distinguished between active and passive euthanasia.
Supreme Court — Active Euthanasia: Characterized as causing death by introducing a new, external agency of harm.
Supreme Court — Passive Euthanasia: Explained as allowing death to occur.
Supreme Court — Euthanasia Debate: Ongoing examination of the issue and its implications on individual rights and medical ethics.
Supreme Court — Right to Die with Dignity: Recognized in a landmark judgment in 2018.

2026-03-12.title: Multi-thousand crore GainBitcoin scam case: CBI arrests Darwin Labs co-founder Ayush Varshney
2026-03-12.title_hi: गेनबिटकॉइन घोटाला मामले में सीबीआई ने डार्विन लैब्स के सह-संस्थापक आयुष वर्ष्णेय को गिरफ्तार किया
2026-03-12.gs_paper: GS2 — Governance: Government Investigations and Regulation
2026-03-12.context: The GainBitcoin scam case involves multiple FIRs registered in various parts of the country, with the CBI conducting the investigation pursuant to the Supreme Court order. The scam is estimated to be worth thousands of crores. The CBI is examining the role of various individuals and companies, including Darwin Labs, in the scam. The investigation is ongoing, with the CBI working to unravel the complex web of transactions and entities involved. The Supreme Court's order has given the CBI the authority to investigate the case and bring those responsible to justice.
key_points: CBI — Investigation: Arrested Darwin Labs co-founder Ayush Varshney in connection with the GainBitcoin scam case.
Supreme Court — Order: Directed the CBI to investigate the case, dated December 13, 2023.
CBI — Investigation: Examining the role of various individuals and companies, including Darwin Labs, in the scam.
CBI — Action: Working to unravel the complex web of transactions and entities involved in the scam.
Supreme Court — Order: Given the CBI the authority to investigate the case and bring those responsible to justice.

2026-03-12.title: Cabinet clears amendments to insolvency, companies laws
2026-03-12.title_hi: कैबिनेट ने दिवाला और दिवालियापन संहिता और कंपनी अधिनियम में संशोधनों को मंजूरी दी
2026-03-12.gs_paper: GS3 — Economy: Corporate Governance and Insolvency
2026-03-12.context: The Cabinet has approved various amendments to the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code and the Companies Act, 2013. The amendments aim to improve the insolvency resolution process and provide more clarity on the rights of creditors. The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, which was introduced in 2016, has been amended several times since its inception. The Companies Act, 2013, is a comprehensive legislation that regulates the incorporation, management, and winding up of companies. The amendments are expected to have a significant impact on the corporate sector and the economy as a whole.
key_points: Cabinet — Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code: Cleared amendments to improve insolvency resolution process.
Cabinet — Companies Act, 2013: Approved amendments to provide more clarity on the rights of creditors.
Ministry of Corporate Affairs — Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code: Will implement the amendments to improve the corporate sector.
Cabinet — Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code: Aims to provide a framework for the resolution of insolvency and bankruptcy cases.
Ministry of Corporate Affairs — Companies Act, 2013: Will ensure that the amendments are implemented effectively.

2026-03-12.title: SRMIST signs MoU with Naval Academy to collaborate in technical education and research
2026-03-12.title_hi: एसआरएमआईएसटी ने नौसेना अकादमी के साथ तकनीकी शिक्षा और अनुसंधान में सहयोग के लिए समझौता ज्ञापन पर हस्ताक्षर किए
2026-03-12.gs_paper: GS3 — Science & Tech: Education and Research
2026-03-12.context: The SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the Naval Academy to collaborate in technical education and research. The institutions will collaborate in academic and research activities, including exchange of faculty, students, and scholarly resources. They will also organise joint seminars, workshops, conferences, and expert lectures. This collaboration aims to enhance the quality of education and research in the field of technology. The partnership will provide opportunities for students and faculty to engage in joint research projects and exchange programs.
key_points: SRMIST — Naval Academy MoU: Collaboration in technical education and research.
SRMIST — Naval Academy MoU: Exchange of faculty, students, and scholarly resources.
SRMIST — Naval Academy MoU: Organisation of joint seminars, workshops, conferences, and expert lectures.
SRMIST — Naval Academy MoU: Enhancement of education and research quality in technology.
SRMIST — Naval Academy MoU: Opportunities for joint research projects and exchange programs.

2026-03-12.title: RBI conducts G-Secs purchase of ₹50,000 crore to inject liquidity, another tranche on Friday
2026-03-12.title_hi: आरबीआई ने ₹50,000 करोड़ की जी-सेक्स खरीद की
2026-03-12.gs_paper: GS3 — Economy: Monetary Policy and RBI
2026-03-12.context: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has conducted a purchase of government securities (G-Secs) worth ₹50,000 crore to inject liquidity into the market. This move comes as the rupee sank to a record low intra-day due to surging crude oil prices, which exceeded $110 a barrel. The RBI's action is aimed at stabilizing the currency and maintaining liquidity in the financial system. The central bank has also announced another tranche of G-Secs purchase, scheduled to take place on Friday. The total amount of liquidity injected through these purchases is expected to have a positive impact on the economy.
key_points: RBI — G-Secs Purchase: ₹50,000 crore to inject liquidity into the market.
RBI — G-Secs Purchase: Another tranche scheduled for Friday.
RBI — Monetary Policy: Aimed at stabilizing the currency and maintaining liquidity.
RBI — Liquidity Management: Total amount of liquidity injected expected to have a positive impact on the economy.
RBI — Currency Management: Efforts to mitigate the effects of rising crude oil prices and currency fluctuations.

2026-03-12.title: Next instalment of Kisan Nidhi to reach farmers on March 13
2026-03-12.title_hi: किसान निधि की अगली किश्त 13 मार्च को किसानों के खाते में आएगी
2026-03-12.gs_paper: GS2 — Governance: Government Schemes and Initiatives
2026-03-12.context: The 22nd instalment of the Kisan Nidhi scheme is set to be released on March 13, with over 2.15 crore women farmers receiving financial assistance. The Agriculture Minister announced this development, highlighting the scheme's focus on supporting women in agriculture. The Kisan Nidhi scheme has been a key initiative of the government to provide financial support to farmers. With this instalment, the scheme continues to empower women farmers across the country. The disbursement of funds is expected to have a positive impact on the agricultural sector.
key_points: Agriculture Ministry — Kisan Nidhi: 22nd instalment to be released on March 13.
Agriculture Ministry — Kisan Nidhi: Over 2.15 crore women farmers to receive financial assistance.

2026-03-12.title: Uptake of induction stoves sluggish in low-income Indian households
2026-03-12.title_hi: भारतीय घरों में इंडक्शन स्टोव की धीमी गति
2026-03-12.gs_paper: GS3 — Economy: Energy Efficiency and Conservation
2026-03-12.context: The use of induction cooktops in Indian households has been slow to gain traction, particularly in low-income households. According to experts, only 5% of Indian households have electric penetration in their kitchens, indicating a lack of adoption of electric cooking methods. This is despite the potential benefits of induction cooking, including energy efficiency and safety. The slow uptake of induction stoves is a concern for policymakers, as it hinders the transition to cleaner and more efficient cooking methods. The Indian government has been promoting the use of electric cooking methods, but more needs to be done to encourage adoption in low-income households.
key_points: Experts — Induction Cooktops: Only 5% of Indian households have electric penetration in their kitchens.
Indian Government — National Mission on Enhanced Energy Efficiency: Promoting the use of energy-efficient appliances, including induction cooktops.
Policymakers — Electric Cooking Methods: Concerned about the slow uptake of induction stoves in low-income households.
Induction Cooking — Energy Efficiency: Offers potential benefits, including energy efficiency and safety.
Low-Income Households — Induction Stoves: Face barriers to adoption, including high upfront costs and lack of awareness.

2026-03-12.title: Iran says it will target U.S.-Israeli economic, banking interests in region
2026-03-12.title_hi: इरान ने क्षेत्र में अमेरिकी-इज़राइली आर्थिक, बैंकिंग हितों पर निशाना साधने की घोषणा की
2026-03-12.context: The announcement comes after an overnight attack on Iranian Bank Sepah in Tehran. The bank is one of the largest in Iran, with significant economic and financial interests in the region. The attack has raised concerns about the escalating tensions between Iran, the U.S., and Israel. The U.S. and Israel have been imposing economic sanctions on Iran, affecting its banking and financial sector. The Iranian government has vowed to retaliate against these sanctions by targeting U.S.-Israeli economic and banking interests in the region.
key_points: Iranian government — Economic Sanctions: Imposing retaliatory measures against U.S.-Israeli economic interests.
U.S. government — Economic Sanctions: Imposed sanctions on Iran in 2018, affecting its banking and financial sector.
Iranian Bank Sepah — Attack: Hit overnight in Tehran, raising concerns about escalating tensions.
Iranian government — Retaliation: Vowed to target U.S.-Israeli economic and banking interests in the region.
U.S. and Israel — Economic Interests: Have significant economic and banking interests in the region, which Iran has threatened to target.

2026-03-12.title_hi: जॉन डीरे ने 130 एचपी ट्रैक्टर ₹65 लाख में लॉन्च किया
2026-03-12.gs_paper: GS3 — Economy: Agriculture and Rural Development
2026-03-12.context: John Deere has launched a new tractor with 130 HP. 2026-03-12.title: CM to lay foundation stone for Phase 1 of ₹3,509-cr. Marine Eco City project in Kochi
2026-03-12.title_hi: मुख्यमंत्री कोच्चि में ₹3,509 करोड़ के मैरीन ईको सिटी परियोजना के पहले चरण का शिलान्यास करेंगे
2026-03-12.gs_paper: GS2 — Governance: Government Schemes and Initiatives
2026-03-12.context: The Marine Eco City project in Kochi is a significant development initiative with a total cost of ₹3,509 crore. The project aims to transform the city's landscape and provide various amenities to its residents. The first phase of the project is expected to bring about substantial changes to the area. The Kerala government has been working towards making Kochi a major hub for trade and commerce. The Marine Eco City project is a part of this effort, with a focus on sustainability and environmental conservation.

2026-03-13.title: Kerala launches comprehensive programme for managing paediatric renal issues
2026-03-13.title_hi: केरल ने बाल्य रोगियों में गुर्दे की बीमारियों के प्रबंधन के लिए व्यापक कार्यक्रम शुरू किया
2026-03-13.gs_paper: GS2 — Social Justice: Healthcare and Welfare Schemes
2026-03-13.context: The programme aims to provide specialized care to children suffering from renal diseases. The SAT Hospital will serve as the central hub for paediatric nephrology in the state, providing advanced medical care and treatment. The hospital will have a team of expert nephrologists and other medical professionals to cater to the needs of paediatric patients. The programme will also focus on creating awareness about renal diseases among children and providing training to medical professionals.
key_points: Kerala Government — Paediatric Nephrology Programme: Launched a comprehensive programme for managing paediatric renal issues.

2026-03-13.title: ISRO, AIIMS sign MoU for cooperation in space medicine and research
2026-03-13.title_hi: इसरो और एम्स ने अंतरिक्ष चिकित्सा और अनुसंधान में सहयोग के लिए समझौता ज्ञापन पर हस्ताक्षर किए
2026-03-13.gs_paper: GS3 — Science & Tech: Space Technology and ISRO
2026-03-13.context: The ISRO and AIIMS signed an MoU to promote joint research in space medicine and research. The memorandum aims to advance human health, performance, and safety during human space missions. ISRO's priority areas will be the focus of the joint research. The space agency will work with AIIMS to achieve the shared objective of improving human health in space missions.
key_points: ISRO — Space Medicine Research: Joint research with AIIMS to advance human health in space missions.
AIIMS — Space Medicine Research: Collaboration with ISRO to improve performance and safety in human space missions.
ISRO — Gaganyaan Mission: Aim to launch the first manned mission by 2023.
ISRO — Research Priority Areas: Focus on joint research with AIIMS in priority areas.
AIIMS — Space Medicine Expertise: Providing expertise to ISRO for human space missions.

2026-03-13.title: Parental income alone cannot decide OBC creamy layer status: Supreme Court
2026-03-13.title_hi: माता-पिता की आय से अकेले ओबीसी क्रीमी लेयर का निर्धारण नहीं हो सकता : सुप्रीम कोर्ट
2026-03-13.gs_paper: GS2 — Social Justice: Other Backward Classes and Reservation Policy
2026-03-13.context: The Supreme Court has ruled in favour of children of senior PSU officials selected as OBC candidates in civil service exams, stating that parental income alone cannot decide OBC creamy layer status. The creamy layer exclusion framework says income from salaries, farm land cannot be included for wealth test. This decision is significant as it clarifies the criteria for determining the creamy layer status of OBC candidates. The Court's ruling is based on the idea that the creamy layer concept is meant to exclude those who are already affluent and do not need reservation benefits. The decision is expected to impact many OBC candidates who were earlier excluded from reservation benefits due to their parental income.
key_points: Supreme Court — OBC Creamy Layer: Parental income alone cannot decide creamy layer status.
Government — Creamy Layer Exclusion Framework: Income from salaries, farm land cannot be included for wealth test.
Supreme Court — Civil Service Exams: Children of senior PSU officials selected as OBC candidates are eligible for reservation benefits.
Government — Reservation Policy: The creamy layer concept is meant to exclude those who are already affluent and do not need reservation benefits.
Supreme Court — OBC Reservation: The decision is expected to impact many OBC candidates who were earlier excluded from reservation benefits due to their parental income.

2026-03-13.title: Farooq Abdullah visits Iran’s Embassy in New Delhi, signs condolence book
2026-03-13.title_hi: फारूक अब्दुल्ला ने ईरान के दूतावास में संवेदना पुस्तिका पर हस्ताक्षर किए
2026-03-13.context: Dr. Farooq Abdullah, accompanied by National Conference (NC) Members of Parliament (MPs), visited Iran's Embassy in New Delhi. The MPs included Chowdry Mohammad Ramzan, Sajjad Ahmed Kichloo, and Gurwinder Singh Oberoi. This visit was to sign a condolence book. The event is significant as it shows diplomatic relations between India and Iran. The National Conference is a political party in Jammu and Kashmir.
key_points: National Conference — Condolence Visit: Dr. Farooq Abdullah signed a condolence book at Iran's Embassy.
National Conference — Diplomatic Efforts: The visit is part of ongoing diplomatic efforts between India and Iran.
Iran's Embassy — Condolence Book: The book was signed by Dr. Abdullah and accompanying MPs.
National Conference — Members of Parliament: Accompanying MPs included Chowdry Mohammad Ramzan, Sajjad Ahmed Kichloo, and Gurwinder Singh Oberoi.
India-Iran Relations — Diplomatic Ties: The visit highlights the ongoing diplomatic relations between the two countries.

2026-03-13.title: Thrissur achieves full housing coverage under LIFE Mission
2026-03-13.title_hi: थ्रिस्सुर जिले में लाइफ मिशन के तहत पूरी हुई घरों की आवश्यकता
2026-03-13.gs_paper: GS2 — Governance: Government Schemes and Initiatives
2026-03-13.context: The LIFE Mission, a housing scheme initiated by the Kerala government, aims to provide housing support to all eligible beneficiaries. Thrissur district has become the first to achieve full housing coverage under this mission, with all eligible beneficiaries in the homeless-with-land category receiving housing support. The district has achieved this milestone through the implementation of the LIFE Mission, which provides financial assistance to beneficiaries to construct their own homes. The mission has been successful in providing housing support to thousands of beneficiaries across the state. The Kerala government has allocated significant funds for the implementation of the LIFE Mission, with a focus on providing housing support to the most vulnerable sections of society.
key_points: Kerala Government — LIFE Mission: Achieved full housing coverage in Thrissur district.
Kerala Government — LIFE Mission: Provided housing support to all eligible beneficiaries in the homeless-with-land category.
Kerala Government — LIFE Mission:Allocated significant funds for the implementation of the mission.
Kerala Government — LIFE Mission: Focus on providing housing support to the most vulnerable sections of society.
Kerala Government — LIFE Mission: Launched in 2016, with the aim of providing housing support to all eligible beneficiaries in the state.

2026-03-13.title: ASEAN Ministers to hold meetings to address West Asia crisis
2026-03-13.title_hi: आसियान मंत्री पश्चिम एशिया संकट को संबोधित करने के लिए बैठकें आयोजित करेंगे
2026-03-13.gs_paper: GS2 — International Relations: Regional Organizations and Cooperation
2026-03-13.context: The Philippines, as this year's ASEAN chair, is hosting the meetings to discuss the impacts and responses to the crisis. The meetings aim to address the surging oil prices and disruptions to shipping, logistics, and trade flows across the region's export-reliant economies. The ASEAN region is heavily dependent on exports, and the current crisis is expected to have significant economic implications. The meetings will bring together officials from ASEAN member states to weigh the impacts and responses to the crisis. The region's economy is highly integrated, and the crisis is expected to have far-reaching consequences.
key_points: ASEAN — Meeting: To address the West Asia crisis and its impacts on the region.
The Philippines — ASEAN Chair: Hosting the meetings to discuss responses to the crisis.
ASEAN — Export-reliant economies: Expected to be significantly affected by the surging oil prices and disruptions to trade flows.
ASEAN — Regional economy: Highly integrated, with the crisis expected to have far-reaching consequences.
ASEAN — Member states: To come together to weigh the impacts and responses to the crisis.

2026-03-13.title: Reports of Iran allowing Indian ships through Strait of Hormuz ‘premature’: Centre
2026-03-13.title_hi: भारत के जहाजों को होरमुज जलडमरूमध्य से गुजरने की अनुमति देने की खबरें ‘अतिशयोक्तिपूर्ण’ : केंद्र
2026-03-13.context: The Indian government has been expressing concern about the targeting of merchant navy ships that are stuck both inside the Gulf as well as in the Gulf of Oman near the Strait of Hormuz. The Strait of Hormuz is a critical shipping lane, with nearly 20% of the world's oil passing through it. The Centre's statement comes amid rising tensions in the region. India has been in talks with Iran to ensure the safe passage of its ships. The Indian Navy has also been deployed in the region to provide security to Indian ships.
key_points: The Centre — Statement: Reports of Iran allowing Indian ships through the Strait of Hormuz are premature.
The Indian Government — Talks: India has been in talks with Iran to ensure the safe passage of its ships.
The Indian Navy — Deployment: The Indian Navy has been deployed in the region to provide security to Indian ships.
The Strait of Hormuz — Importance: Nearly 20% of the world's oil passes through the Strait of Hormuz.
The Region — Tensions: The region has seen rising tensions in recent years, with Iran and the US having differing views on the shipping lane.

2026-03-13.title: Lacking long-term storage, India must rely on imports to manage LPG supply
2026-03-13.title_hi: भारत को एलपीजी आपूर्ति प्रबंधन के लिए आयात पर निर्भर रहना होगा
2026-03-13.gs_paper: GS3 — Economy: Energy Security and Trade
2026-03-13.context: India's LPG production was 13 million tonnes in 2024-25, according to government data. In the same year, LPG consumption was around 31 million tonnes. This significant gap between production and consumption has resulted in a 60-40 import-export split. The country's lack of long-term storage facilities exacerbates the issue, making it challenging to manage LPG supply. As a result, India must rely heavily on imports to meet its LPG demands.
key_points: Government — LPG Production: 13 million tonnes in 2024-25.
Government — LPG Consumption: 31 million tonnes in 2024-25.
Government — Import-Export Split: 60-40.
Government — Storage Capacity: Lack of long-term storage facilities.
Government — Import Reliance: Heavy reliance on imports to meet LPG demands.

2026-03-13.title: Iran says it will target U.S.-Israeli economic, banking interests in region
2026-03-13.title_hi: इरान ने अमेरिका-इज़राइल के आर्थिक और बैंकिंग हितों पर हमला करने की घोषणा की
2026-03-13.context: The announcement comes after an overnight attack on Iranian Bank Sepah in Tehran. The bank is one of the largest in Iran, with significant economic and banking interests in the region. The U.S. and Israel have been accused of being behind the attack, which has led to a significant escalation of tensions in the region. The attack on Bank Sepah is seen as a major blow to Iran's economy, with potential implications for the country's banking sector. The U.S. and Israel have not commented on the attack, but Iran has vowed to retaliate against their economic and banking interests.
key_points: Iran — Economic Interests: Will target U.S.-Israeli economic interests in the region.
Iran — Banking Interests: Will target U.S.-Israeli banking interests in the region.
U.S. — Sanctions: Has imposed significant economic sanctions on Iran.
Israel — Involvement: Accused of being behind the attack on Bank Sepah.
Bank Sepah — Attack: Was hit overnight in Tehran, with significant implications for Iran's economy.

2026-03-13.title: Oil prices plunge to $87.8 per barrel as IEA eyes emergency release with Strait of Hormuz in focus
2026-03-13.title_hi: अंतर्राष्ट्रीय ऊर्जा एजेंसी की आपातकालीन तेल भंडार रिलीज़ पर नज़र, $87.8 प्रति बैरल पर तेल की कीमतें गिरीं
2026-03-13.gs_paper: GS3 — Economy: Energy Security and Policy
2026-03-13.context: The IEA executive director stated that he is in close contact with energy ministers from key energy producers and consumers after a meeting of G7 energy ministers in Paris. The price of oil has fallen to $87.8 per barrel. The IEA is monitoring the situation in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil shipping route. The G7 meeting discussed the current energy market situation and potential responses to price volatility. The IEA's emergency release of oil reserves is an option being considered to stabilize the market.
key_points: IEA — Emergency Oil Release: Considering release of oil reserves to stabilize the market.
G7 Energy Ministers — Paris Meeting: Discussed current energy market situation and potential responses to price volatility.
IEA — Oil Price Monitoring: Monitoring the situation in the Strait of Hormuz and its impact on oil prices.
IEA — Energy Minister Contacts: IEA executive director in close contact with energy ministers from key energy producers and consumers.
Oil Market — Price Drop: Oil prices have fallen to $87.8 per barrel.
